ECON325 Intermediate Macroeconomic Analysis (4 Credits)

Analysis of macroeconomic behavior and policy with emphasis on theoretical rigor. Topics include the determinants of economic growth, unemployment, inflation, and international economic flows. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in ECON200 and ECON201; and minimum grade of C- in ECON300 or (MATH241 and any statistics course).

Restriction: Must be in one of the following programs (Economics Bachelor of Science; Economics minor).

Credit Only Granted for. ECON305 or ECON325.
